content_markdown:
A standing (stationary) wave is set up on a string at a particular frequency as shown.

How many nodes will be on the string if the frequency is doubled but nothing else is changed?

diagram_metadata.description: A standing wave on a string fixed at both ends showing three loops (the third harmonic).

vibe_explanation: The initial diagram shows the 3rd harmonic ($n=3$), which has 4 nodes (including the ends). Since $v = f\lambda$ and the wave speed $v$ is constant, doubling the frequency $f$ halves the wavelength $\lambda$. This means the number of loops doubles from 3 to 6. A standing wave with 6 loops (the 6th harmonic) has $n+1$ nodes, which is $6+1=7$ nodes.
